rationale: Both candidates miss most of the actual ground-truth concerns (doc clarification on kernels versions, test-mark/skipif process nits, and the confirmed FA3 SM90+ gating bug), and both independently catch the same real issues (masked-path crash when only one flash-attn function is present, and heavy FA2/FA3 code duplication). Y's precision edges ahead because it pinpoints a directly diff-verifiable bug — the third `get_kernel()` fallback call has no try/except and will raise unhandled — whereas X's parallel finding on the same code region (claiming `version=1/2` ints are silently no-op'd per PEP440 semantics) is speculative and arguably contradicted by the author's own clarification that these are just git-branch identifiers. Y also runs a transparent validated/sweep pass (including checking other platforms) that adds credibility, though it incorrectly clears the FA3 Hopper-gating logic as correct when that was in fact a real bug fixed post-review. Both are similarly actionable with concrete file/line references and fixes.
